title: Blephariglottis × lueri
taxonomy: Blephariglottis × lueri is a hybrid orchid plant, specifically a cross between Blephariglottis and another Blephariglottis species. This hybrid orchid is classified under the family Orchidaceae. As a hybrid orchid, it is not assigned a formal species classification but is rather identified by its parent species. The classification of Blephariglottis × lueri is often noted in botanical databases and orchid catalogs. The exact classification can vary depending on the source and the specific parent species involved.

intro: Blephariglottis × lueri, also known as Luer's Pleurothallis, is a hybrid orchid species characterized by its distinctive flowers and compact growth habit. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in South America, to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Blephariglottis × lueri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Blephariglottis × lueri is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in infusions and decoctions to leverage its potential health benefits.
